name: Jaén and Martos Olive Groves Loop
name_original: Jaén – vuelta por Torredelcampo y Martos
description: This difficult 38.8-mile (62.5 km) road cycling route from Jaén explores Spain's olive oil region with 3416 feet of climbing.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Martos are set within the province of Jaén, Andalusia, characterized by extensive olive groves and rolling hills. The terrain includes the dramatic limestone peak of La Peña de Martos and the hilly topography of the Sierra Jabalcuz mountain range. The region offers varied gradients, from gentle slopes to challenging ascents, suitable for road cyclists. The Vía Verde del Aceite, a former railway line, provides a relatively flat and accessible path through the area. Best road cycling routes around Martos The most popular road cycling route is Martos – tour around Fuensanta de Martos and Los Villares, a 30.2 miles (48.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 35 minutes to complete. This difficult route navigates through the local towns and surrounding agricultural landscapes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Martos, Santiago de Calatrava, and Torredonjimeno Loop, a difficult 42.9 miles (69.1 km) path. This route connects several towns, offering a diverse cycling experience across varied terrain. Local road cyclists also love the Jaén and Martos Olive Groves Loop, a 38.8 miles (62.5 km) trail leading through extensive olive groves, often completed in about 3 hours 11 minutes. Road cycling around Martos is defined by endless olive groves, rolling hills, and the limestone peak of La Peña de Martos. The network offers options for different ability levels, from accessible paths to demanding climbs. The routes in Martos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 3 reviews. More than 500 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Martos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Martos?
A: Road cycling around Martos is characterized by extensive olive groves and rolling hills, typical of Jaén province. You'll encounter varied gradients, from gentle slopes perfect for leisure to more challenging ascents in the Sierra Jabalcuz mountain range. The dramatic limestone peak of La Peña de Martos also offers significant climbs and panoramic views.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Martos?
A: Yes, Martos offers options for different ability levels. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996247342/martos-tour-around-fuensanta-de-martos-and-los-villares" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Martos – tour around Fuensanta de Martos and Los Villares&lt;/a&gt; route is a good starting point. Additionally, the Vía Verde del Aceite, a former railway line, provides a relatively flat and accessible path ideal for leisure cycling and families, offering a safe, traffic-free environment.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Martos area?
A: There are over 45 road cycling routes around Martos documented on komoot, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. This includes 1 easy route, 18 moderate routes, and 28 difficult routes.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or points of interest I can see while cycling around Martos?
A: The region is rich in historical and natural landmarks. You can cycle past the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5312371" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of the Peña of Martos&lt;/a&gt;, or explore routes that offer views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2876708" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro del Tambor Summit&lt;/a&gt;. Other routes might take you near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5286708" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Santa Catalina&lt;/a&gt; or the historic city of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4040101" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jaén&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Martos?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from 3 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, from the serene olive groves to the challenging mountain climbs, and the picturesque views of the surrounding countryside.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes from Martos?
A: Yes, for those seeking longer distances, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38243240" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascante Tower – View of Alcalá la Real loop from Antigua Estacion Tren de Martos&lt;/a&gt; cover over 113 km, offering extended cycling experiences through varied landscapes and connecting to other towns.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes around Martos?
A: Many of the routes around Martos are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009120658/martos-santiago-de-calatrava-and-torredonjimeno-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Martos, Santiago de Calatrava, and Torredonjimeno Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which connects several towns, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996044828/jaen-and-martos-olive-groves-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jaén and Martos Olive Groves Loop&lt;/a&gt;, offering scenic rides through the region's signature landscape.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Martos?
A: Martos, located in Andalusia, generally offers good cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures for cycling. Summers can be very hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended during those months. Winters are typically mild, making it possible to cycle year-round, though some higher elevation routes might be cooler.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. Martos is situated at the western end of the Sierra Jabalcuz mountain range, providing plenty of opportunities for demanding cycling routes with significant ascents and descents. The region has even been a stage town for La Vuelta ciclista a España, highlighting its suitability for high-level road cycling. Many routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40365352" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jaén Cathedral – Río Frío Spring loop&lt;/a&gt;, feature over 1300 meters of elevation gain.

Q: What are the main natural features I will encounter on road cycling routes in Martos?
A: The dominant natural features are the endless olive groves that blanket the rolling hills of Jaén, creating a unique and picturesque backdrop. You will also encounter the dramatic limestone peak of La Peña de Martos, which offers stunning views, and the hilly topography of the Sierra Jabalcuz mountain range, providing varied and challenging terrain.

Q: Are there any routes that specifically showcase the olive groves?
A: Yes, the region is famous for its olive oil production, and many routes immerse you in this iconic landscape. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996044828/jaen-and-martos-olive-groves-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jaén and Martos Olive Groves Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, leading you directly through extensive olive groves for a truly characteristic experience of the area.
tips: The Castle of Santa Catilina in Jaén is located on a promontory visible from many kilometers around. In that same place is located the National Tourist Parador of Jaén, which preserves all the aesthetics of the environment.
The Torredelcampo station is perhaps one of the best preserved old railway stations on the entire Vía Verde del Aceite.
The city of Martos is dominated by the imposing steep rock known as the "Peña de Martos" which casts its shadow on the city below.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Jaén – return trip via Torredelcampo and Martos route?
faqs.answer: This challenging road cycling route is approximately 62.5 kilometers (38.8 miles) long. Komoot estimates the duration to be around 3 hours and 11 minutes, but this can vary significantly based on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery or visit landmarks.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is categorized as a difficult road bike tour. With an elevation gain of over 1,040 meters, it demands a very good level of fitness and is best suited for experienced cyclists looking for a challenging ride.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily follows paved roads, but some sections, particularly those that might coincide with or run parallel to the Vía Verde del Aceite, could have loose ground. While the Vía Verde is generally smooth, be prepared for varied surfaces, which might require careful handling on a road bike.

faqs.question: What landmarks and viewpoints can I expect to see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several notable points of interest. These include the impressive View of the Castle of Santa Catalina and Jaén City Walls, the historic Torredelcampo Old Train Station, and the majestic Peña de Martos Castle. The route also offers numerous viewpoints overlooking the vast olive groves of Jaén.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery will I experience while cycling this route?
faqs.answer: The route immerses you in the quintessential landscape of Jaén province, the world's largest olive oil-producing region. You'll cycle through endless olive groves, dramatic mountain landscapes, and pass by historical elements like old viaducts and recovered tunnels that are part of the former railway line of the Vía Verde del Aceite. Expect picturesque whitewashed villages and spectacular vistas over the Andalusian olive landscape, a UNESCO World Heritage site.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas aren't detailed, as the route starts and ends in Jaén, you would typically look for public parking within the city. Consider parking near the starting point in Jaén to easily access the route. Public transport options to Jaén are also available if you're not driving.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The ideal times to cycle this route are during spring (March-May) and autumn (September-November). Temperatures are pleasant, and the olive groves are particularly beautiful. Summers can be extremely hot, often exceeding 35°C, so if cycling in summer, it's best to start very early in the morning.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to cycle this route. It utilizes public roads and sections of the Vía Verde del Aceite, which are freely accessible.

faqs.question: Is this route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route primarily follows public roads, which are generally accessible, some sections might be part of the Vía Verde del Aceite. If cycling with a dog, ensure it is well-behaved and kept on a leash, especially in populated areas or near traffic. Always carry water for your pet.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for refreshments or coffee along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through towns like Torredelcampo and Martos, you will find opportunities to stop for refreshments, coffee, or a meal. These towns offer local cafes and establishments where cyclists can take a break.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other well-known cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers sections of the Camino Mozárabe desde Jaén and the Camino Natural Vía Verde del Aceite: Jaén a rio Guadajoz. You'll also find smaller segments of trails like [CIMA JA06] Castillo de Sasnta Catalina and [CIMA JA11] La Pandera * Los Villares.

waypoints.name: View of the Castle of Santa Catalina and Jaén City Walls
